chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ جَعَلَ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ صَوْماً مَعْلُوماً وَ مَنْ نَذَرَ أَنْ يَصُومَ فِي شُكْرٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 58 – The one who makes it upon himself specified Fasts, and the one who vows that he would be Fasting in appreciation

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/350)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/350)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنِّي جَعَلْتُ عَلَى نَفْسِي أَنْ أَصُومَ حَتَّى يَقُومَ الْقَائِمُ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ صُمْ وَ لَا تَصُمْ فِي السَّفَرِ وَ لَا الْعِيدَيْنِ وَ لَا أَيَّامَ التَّشْرِيقِ وَ لَا الْيَوْمَ الَّذِي يُشَكُّ فِيهِ مِنْ شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Karram who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I made it upon myself that I would be Fasting until the rising of Al-Qaim<sup>asws</sup>’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Fast, and do not Fast during the journey, nor the two Eids, nor the days of Al-Tashreek (11<sup>th</sup>, 12<sup>th</sup> & 13<sup>th</sup> of Zil-Hijja), nor the day wherein is a doubt from a Month of Ramazan’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Karram who has said the following: “Once, I said to abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that I have placed upon myself to fast until the rise of al-Qa”im, (the one who rises with Divine Authority). He (the Imam) said, ’Fast but do not fast on a journey, or the two ‘Id, the days of tashriq (11, 12 and 13) and the day which is doubtful about its being of the month of Ramadan.”’
